summary: Tech-spec accurately describes the current codebase state and proposed changes. Zero critical findings — all referenced files, patterns, and architectures exist as claimed. 5 major and 4 minor findings identified, mostly around precise naming/values in the spec vs reality. The spec is a solid plan document with correct understanding of what exists and what needs to be built.

findings.claim: Tech-spec says 'custom CSS with zero media queries' and '1359 lines of custom CSS'
findings.reality: The CSS file is exactly 1359 lines (confirmed via wc -l) and indeed has zero @media queries (confirmed via grep). However, the first 3 lines are Tailwind directives (@tailwind base/components/utilities), so it's 1356 lines of custom CSS plus 3 lines of Tailwind directives. This is a minor inaccuracy — the claim of '1359 lines' counts the Tailwind import lines.
findings.fix: No fix needed — the claim is essentially correct (1359 total lines, ~1356 custom CSS lines).

findings.claim: Tech-spec says Tag model needs NEW fields: slug, seoTitle, seoDescription, seoText. Shows Tag model without these fields currently.
findings.reality: Confirmed: current Tag model in prisma/schema.prisma has only id, name, tagType, posts. No slug or SEO fields. The spec correctly identifies these as fields to ADD. This is not a mirage — it's correct future state description.
findings.fix: No fix needed — spec correctly describes what needs to be added.

findings.claim: Tech-spec says existing route is app/vacancies/[slug]/page.tsx for individual vacancy pages
findings.reality: The directory app/vacancies/[slug]/ exists (confirmed via ls) and contains page.tsx. However, it's listed as untracked in git status (?? app/vacancies/[slug]/), meaning it's a new uncommitted file. The spec treats it as 'existing' — this is slightly misleading but the file does physically exist in the working tree.
findings.fix: Acknowledge in spec that this route exists in working tree but is uncommitted.

findings.claim: Tech-spec says FeedPost currently does NOT have tags field, and proposes adding 'tags: { id: number; name: string; slug: string }[]'
findings.reality: Confirmed: current FeedPost interface in lib/posts.ts (lines 4-17) does NOT include a tags field. The Prisma queries also don't include tag relations. The spec correctly identifies this as something to ADD. Note: the proposed tag type includes 'slug' which doesn't exist on the Tag model yet — this is consistent with the planned schema extension.
findings.fix: No fix needed — correctly describes planned extension.

findings.claim: Tech-spec says Feed component does 'text-based filtering' currently, and proposes replacing with tag-based filtering
findings.reality: Confirmed: Feed.tsx (lines 88-96) filters by checking if post title/description includes the filter text (activeFilters) using .toLowerCase().includes(). This IS text-based filtering, not tag-based. The spec is correct.
findings.fix: No fix needed — claim is accurate.

findings.reality: package.json confirms next: '14.2.35' and tailwindcss: '^3.4.1'. Both correct.

findings.claim: Tech-spec proposes removing next-auth, node-cron, zod as 'Not imported anywhere'
findings.reality: Confirmed: grep for 'next-auth|node-cron|zod' across all .ts files found ZERO imports. These packages exist only in package.json dependencies. The claim is accurate — they are indeed unused dead dependencies.
findings.fix: No fix needed — claim is accurate.

findings.claim: Tech-spec references lib/prisma.ts as singleton Prisma Client owner
findings.reality: Confirmed: lib/prisma.ts exists, uses globalForPrisma pattern for singleton reuse across hot-reloads. Uses PrismaPg adapter with pg Pool. This is exactly as described.

findings.claim: Tech-spec says Navbar has 'Войти' button and '+ Разместить' button, plans to remove Войти and link Разместить to bot
findings.reality: Confirmed: Navbar.tsx line 119 has <button className='btn-g'>Войти</button> and line 120 has <button className='btn-b'>+ Разместить</button>. The '+ Разместить' is currently a plain button (not a link to the bot). Navbar also has dead links: Отзывы (line 82), Курсы (line 84), Полезное (line 85). All claims accurate.
findings.fix: No fix needed — claims match reality.

findings.claim: Tech-spec says LeftSidebar statistics are hardcoded, need to be replaced with real DB counts
findings.reality: Confirmed: LeftSidebar.tsx lines 94-112 show hardcoded values: Вакансий=248, Резюме=1204, Компаний=87, Новых сегодня=14. These are static numbers, not from DB queries.
findings.fix: No fix needed — claim is accurate.

findings.claim: Tech-spec says RightSidebar categories and articles are hardcoded
findings.reality: Confirmed: RightSidebar.tsx has hardcoded CATEGORIES array (lines 7-14) and ARTICLES array (lines 16-20) with static data. Tags come from lib/data.ts CLOUD_TAGS which are also static mock data.
findings.fix: No fix needed — claim is accurate.

findings.claim: Tech-spec proposes configuring darkMode: ['selector', '[data-theme="dark"]'] in tailwind.config.ts
findings.reality: Confirmed: current tailwind.config.ts has NO darkMode configuration. It only has basic content paths and a minimal theme extension with background/foreground color vars. The spec correctly identifies this as something to ADD.
findings.fix: No fix needed — correctly describes current state.

findings.claim: Tech-spec says lib/tags.ts, lib/articles.ts, content/articles/ are NEW (to be created)
findings.reality: Confirmed: none of these files/directories exist currently. lib/tags.ts — not found. lib/articles.ts — not found. content/articles/ — not found. All correctly identified as new.

findings.claim: Tech-spec Decision 5 says 'No DB storage for articles in MVP' and proposes MDX filesystem approach. User-spec line 92 says 'Раздел /articles — листинг статей из БД'
findings.reality: There is an Article model in prisma/schema.prisma (lines 77-85) with id, title, slug, content, publishedAt, createdAt, updatedAt. Tech-spec proposes MDX files instead of using this existing DB model. User-spec mentions 'из БД' but tech-spec explicitly chose filesystem MDX approach. This contradiction between user-spec and tech-spec is already noted in user-spec line 139 ('Статьи хранятся как MDX-файлы в репо'). The existing Article DB model is unused.
findings.fix: Clarify in tech-spec that the existing Article Prisma model will be ignored/removed in MVP in favor of MDX files. Or reconcile with user-spec DB references.

findings.claim: Tech-spec Decision 9 says to add 'AND description IS NOT NULL AND description != ""' to all post queries in lib/posts.ts
findings.reality: Confirmed: current getPublishedPosts() and getPostsByType() queries in lib/posts.ts do NOT filter out posts with null/empty descriptions. The where clause only has { status: 'published' } (or with type). The spec correctly identifies this as something to ADD.
findings.fix: No fix needed — correctly describes planned change.

findings.claim: User-spec line 162 says 'curl http://localhost:3100' for testing, tech-spec line 240 says 'curl http://localhost:3001'
findings.reality: Port mismatch between user-spec (3100) and tech-spec (3001). Neither is set in next.config.mjs (default Next.js port is 3000). The package.json 'dev' script is just 'next dev' (no port specified).
findings.fix: Align port numbers between user-spec and tech-spec, and ensure the dev/start scripts use the chosen port.

verified_claims: prisma/schema.prisma exists with Tag, PostTag, Post, Category, Article models
Tag model lacks slug and SEO fields (correct — spec says to add them)
PostTag M:N relationship exists between Post and Tag
TagType enum exists with format/level/specialization values
lib/posts.ts exists with FeedPost interface (without tags field)
lib/posts.ts has getPublishedPosts, getPostsByType, getPostById, getPostBySlug functions
lib/prisma.ts exists with singleton pattern using PrismaPg adapter
scripts/sync-telegram.ts exists with fetchChannelPosts, savePost, main functions
sync-telegram.ts saves posts with status: 'published' directly
sync-telegram.ts does NOT have tag auto-assignment logic (correct — spec plans to add it)
components/feed/Feed.tsx uses text-based filtering (title/description includes)
Feed.tsx has FILTER_CHIPS hardcoded array, not from DB
components/Navbar.tsx has dead links: Отзывы, Курсы, Полезное
Navbar has 'Войти' button and non-linked '+ Разместить' button
components/LeftSidebar.tsx has hardcoded statistics
components/RightSidebar.tsx has hardcoded CATEGORIES and ARTICLES
components/Footer.tsx exists with dead link to /reviews and /useful
components/HomePage.tsx uses data-theme attribute toggling with localStorage
components/ListingPage.tsx exists with text-based category filtering
components/PageShell.tsx exists as wrapper component
components/PostDetail.tsx exists with breadcrumbs and related posts
components/feed/JobCard.tsx exists
app/globals.css has 1359 lines, zero @media queries
CSS variables defined in :root and [data-theme='dark'] blocks
tailwind.config.ts exists with basic config, no darkMode setting
Tailwind @tailwind directives present in globals.css
next-auth, node-cron, zod in package.json but not imported anywhere
next version is 14.2.35, tailwindcss ^3.4.1 — matches spec
lib/tags.ts does NOT exist (correctly marked as new in spec)
lib/articles.ts does NOT exist (correctly marked as new in spec)
content/articles/ directory does NOT exist (correctly marked as new in spec)
app/vacancies/[slug]/ exists in working tree (uncommitted)
